2606.29859.title: Exploring Motivations for Algorithm Mention in the Domain of Natural Language Processing: A Deep Learning Approach
2606.29859.paper_summary: With the rise of data-intensive science, algorithms have become central to scientific research. In academic papers, algorithms are mentioned for different purposes, such as describing, using, comparing, or improving methods for specific research tasks. Identifying these purposes can reveal relationships among algorithms and help assess their roles and value. Taking natural language processing (NLP) as an example, this study proposes a sentence-level framework for identifying, analyzing, and tracing the evolution of motivations for mentioning algorithms. We first identify algorithm entities and algorithm-related sentences from full-text papers through manual annotation and machine learning. We then classify mention motivations using pretrained models and data augmentation, and analyze their distribution and temporal evolution. The results show that deep learning models trained with augmented data outperform traditional machine learning models in motivation classification. In NLP papers, more than half of algorithm-related sentences express direct use, whereas improvement is the least frequent motivation. The diversity of motivations has increased over time. For specific algorithm categories, grammar-based algorithms are more often mentioned for description, while machine learning algorithms are more often mentioned for use. Over time, use motivations have gradually replaced description motivations across different algorithms, and the number of motivation types associated with individual algorithms has declined significantly. This study reveals how authors mention algorithm entities in academic writing and provides a basis for future research on algorithm relationship identification and algorithm impact evaluation.
2606.29859.paper_summary_zh: 隨著數據密集型科學的興起，算法已成為科學研究的核心。在學術論文中，算法被提及的目的各不相同，例如描述、使用、比較或改進特定研究任務的方法。識別這些目的可以揭示算法之間的關係，並幫助評估它們的角色和價值。以自然語言處理（NLP）為例，本研究提出了一個句子級框架，用於識別、分析和追蹤提及算法的動機演變。我們首先通過手動標註和機器學習從全文論文中識別算法實體和與算法相關的句子。然後，我們使用預訓練模型和數據增強來分類提及動機，並分析它們的分佈和時間演變。結果顯示，使用增強數據訓練的深度學習模型在動機分類上優於傳統機器學習模型。在NLP論文中，超過一半的與算法相關的句子表達了直接使用，而改進則是最不常見的動機。隨著時間的推移，動機的多樣性有所增加。對於特定的算法類別，基於語法的算法更常被提及以進行描述，而機器學習算法則更常被提及以進行使用。隨著時間的推移，使用動機逐漸取代了不同算法的描述動機，與單個算法相關的動機類型數量顯著下降。本研究揭示了作者在學術寫作中如何提及算法實體，並為未來的算法關係識別和算法影響評估研究提供了基礎。
2606.29859.author: Yuzhuo Wang et.al.
2606.29859.authors: Yuzhuo Wang, Yi Xiang, Chengzhi Zhang
